titel:
Stricter requirements for Swedish citizenship.

# Skärpta krav för svenskt medborgarskap
## Bakgrund
Regeringen har beslutat att skärpa kraven för att bli svensk medborgare. Detta innebär bland annat att kraven på språkkunskaper och kunskap om det svenska samhället höjs.

## Nya krav
De nya kraven innebär bland annat följande:

*   **Högre krav på språkkunskaper:** För att bli svensk medborgare krävs nu en högre nivå i svenska språket, både i tal och skrift.
*   **Ökad kunskap om det svenska samhället:** De som ansöker om svenskt medborgarskap måste visa att de har goda kunskaper om det svenska samhället, inklusive dess historia, kultur och värderingar.
*   **Striktare krav på ekonomisk självförsörjning:** För att bli svensk medborgare måste man kunna försörja sig ekonomiskt och inte vara beroende av socialbidrag.
*   **Inga undantag för vissa grupper:** Tidigare har det funnits vissa undantag från kraven för vissa grupper, till exempel personer som har arbetat i Sverige under en längre tid. Dessa undantag har nu tagits bort.

## Syfte
Syftet med de skärpta kraven är att säkerställa att de som blir svenska medborgare har goda kunskaper om det svenska språket och samhället, samt att de kan försörja sig ekonomiskt.

## Kritik
De skärpta kraven har mötts av kritik från flera håll. Vissa menar att kraven är för höga och att de kommer att göra det svårare för människor att bli svenska medborgare. Andra menar att kraven är nödvändiga för att säkerställa att de som blir svenska medborgare är väl integrerade i det svenska samhället.

## Framtiden
Det är ännu oklart hur de skärpta kraven kommer att påverka antalet personer som blir svenska medborgare. Det är dock troligt att antalet kommer att minska.
html:
It is important that Swedish citizenship is given great significance. The value of Swedish citizenship must be respected and upheld to a greater extent than it is today. Therefore, the government is proposing changes that aim to tighten the rules regarding the acquisition of Swedish citizenship, and these changes essentially involve the following:

  • The requirements for residency in Sweden are being tightened.
  • Higher requirements are being placed on the lifestyle of those who wish to acquire Swedish citizenship.
  • A requirement for financial self-sufficiency is being introduced when applying for citizenship.
  • A requirement for sufficient knowledge of Swedish and of Swedish society is being introduced in order to obtain Swedish citizenship.
  • The application process is being streamlined as much as possible.
  • It is being made possible for children to independently apply for and acquire Swedish citizenship.

In order to ensure that Swedish law is in compliance with EU law, it is also proposed that former Swedish citizens who were born outside of Sweden and have never resided in the country should be able to apply to regain their Swedish citizenship.

The proposed changes to the law are scheduled to come into effect on June 6, 2026. However, the changes regarding the citizenship test in reading and listening comprehension in Swedish at a functional level are proposed to come into effect on October 1, 2027, or on an earlier date determined by the government. The changes regarding the citizenship test in Swedish in other respects are proposed to come into effect on a date determined by the government.

Proposed Resolutions of the Riksdag (Parliament)

The government's proposals:

  1. The Riksdag approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2001:82) on Swedish citizenship.
  2. The Riksdag approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2001:82) on Swedish citizenship.
  3. The Riksdag approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2001:82) on Swedish citizenship.
  4. The Riksdag approves the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2014:481) amending the Act (2001:82) on Swedish citizenship.

Prop. 2025/26:175 5
tldr: The government intends to make it more difficult to become a Swedish citizen by introducing new requirements, including those related to finances, language proficiency, and the length of time a person has resided in the country. The proposed changes are scheduled to take effect in June 2026.
lattlast:
The government has proposed changes to the rules for obtaining Swedish citizenship. They want to make it more important and difficult to become a citizen of Sweden. This means that applicants must meet new and stricter requirements.

The new rules mean that applicants must have lived in Sweden for a longer period and must be able to support themselves, meaning they must have sufficient funds to live. It will also require a better knowledge of the Swedish language and an understanding of how Swedish society functions. The government will also consider how applicants live their lives.

There are also proposals that would allow children to apply for citizenship independently. At the same time, the government proposes that individuals born abroad who were previously Swedish citizens should be able to apply to have their citizenship restored. This is being done to ensure that Swedish laws are in line with EU regulations.

Most of these changes are planned to come into effect on June 6, 2026. Some parts of the rules, such as the Swedish language test, may come into effect later, for example, on October 1, 2027.
